1215	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1: the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition
1216	
1217	These are general `PresheafOfModules` statements (any `F : C ⥤ D`, ring presheaves
1218	`R, S`), not specific to schemes; they are project-local because Mathlib's pinned commit
1219	exposes neither `extendScalars` nor `pullback₀` at the presheaf-of-modules level. Both are
1220	realised as the left adjoint of an existing right adjoint: `pushforward₀ F R` is
1221	definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))` (because `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose,
1222	witnessed by Mathlib's `restrictScalars (𝟙 R)).Full := inferInstanceAs (𝟭 _).Full`), and
1223	`restrictScalars φ` is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`; since `pushforward _` is
1224	always a right adjoint, so are these two, and their left adjoints `pullback₀`/`extendScalars`
1225	exist. The decomposition `pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀` then follows from the
1226	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ` by
1227	uniqueness of left adjoints (`Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso`).
1228	
1229	Per blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition` (D1). **OFF-PATH (iter-243 pivot).** D1 is
1230	axiom-clean and self-contained, but it was the first brick of the ABANDONED general
1231	strong-monoidal pullback build (`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, route (e)); the active
1232	route is the loc-triv chart-chase D1'–D4' (§below), which does NOT consume `extendScalars`/
1233	`pullback₀`. Retained as a correct, reusable presheaf-level decomposition; do NOT extend it
1234	toward the general build (D2 scalar-strong / D3 topological-interchange are NOT pursued). -/
1235	
1236	section PullbackLanDecomposition
1237	
1238	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1239	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
1240	
1241	/-- `pushforward₀ F R` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))`
1242	(since `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose). Project-local; carries the existence of the
1243	topological inverse image `pullback₀`. -/
1244	private lemma pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1245	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀.{u} F R).IsRightAdjoint :=
1246	  inferInstanceAs (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))).IsRightAdjoint
1247	
1248	/-- `restrictScalars φ` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`.
1249	Project-local; carries the existence of the extension of scalars `extendScalars`. -/
1250	private lemma restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1251	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
1252	  inferInstanceAs
1253	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (F := 𝟭 C) (R := F.op ⋙ R) φ).IsRightAdjoint
1254	
1255	/-- **The topological inverse image `pullback₀ := (pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint`**, the
1256	left adjoint of the fixed-ring pushforward. On underlying presheaves it is the left Kan
1257	extension along `F.op`. Project-local (no `PresheafOfModules` inverse-image functor at the
1258	pin); the carrier of D3. -/
1259	noncomputable def pullback0 (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1260	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} R :=
1261	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1262	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint
1263	
1264	/-- **Extension of scalars `extendScalars φ := (restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint`**, the left
1265	adjoint of restriction of scalars along a morphism of ring presheaves. Project-local; the
1266	carrier of D2 (strong monoidal via `distribBaseChange`). -/
1267	noncomputable def extendScalars (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1268	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} S ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) :=
1269	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1270	  (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint
1271	
1272	/-- The adjunction `pullback₀ ⊣ pushforward₀`. -/
1273	noncomputable def pullback0Adjunction (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1274	    pullback0 F R ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R :=
1275	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1276	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R)
1277	
1278	/-- The adjunction `extendScalars φ ⊣ restrictScalars φ`. -/
1279	noncomputable def extendScalarsAdjunction (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1280	    extendScalars φ ⊣ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ :=
1281	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1282	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ)
1283	
1284	/-- **D1 — the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition** (blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition`).
1285	For `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` presenting a pushforward of presheaves of modules, the presheaf
1286	pullback factors as extension of scalars followed by the topological inverse image,
1287	`pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀`. This is the left-adjoint reversal of the
1288	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, obtained
1289	from `Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso` (uniqueness of left adjoints). Project-local. -/
1290	noncomputable def pullbackLanDecomposition (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1291	    PresheafOfModules.pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback0 F R :=
1292	  (Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
1293	    (extendScalarsAdjunction φ) (pullback0Adjunction F R)
1294	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
1295	    (Iso.refl (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ))).symm
1296	
1297	end PullbackLanDecomposition

1299	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1'–D4' loc-triv pullback–tensor comparison
1300	
1301	The locally-trivial-restricted upgrade of the oplax comparison map

1305	section LocTrivPullbackTensor
1306	
1307	/-- **The sheafified `⊗ₘ` of the two `pullbackValIso`s (piece 4 of `pullbackTensorMap`) is an

1311	private lemma isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
1312	    (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
1313	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1314	      (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1315	        (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1316	        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
1317	        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom))) :=
1318	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1319	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1320	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1321	      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f M))
1322	      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f N)))).isIso_hom

1324	/-- **Reduction of `pullbackTensorMap` iso-ness to the sheafified presheaf δ.**

300	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
301	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
302	
303	\begin{definition}
304	
305	
306	\leanok
307	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
308	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
309	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
310	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
311	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
312	  \[
313	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
314	  \]
315	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
316	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
317	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
318	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
319	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
320	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
321	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
322	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
323	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
324	  canonical compatibility
325	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
326	     \;\cong\;
327	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
328	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
329	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
330	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
331	
332	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
333	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
334	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
335	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
336	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
337	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
338	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
339	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
340	\end{definition}
341	
342	\begin{lemma}
343	
344	
345	\leanok
346	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
347	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
348	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
349	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
350	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
351	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
352	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
353	  \[
354	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
355	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
356	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
357	  \]
358	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
359	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
360	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
361	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
362	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
363	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
364	  \[
365	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
366	    \qquad
367	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
368	  \]
369	  the associator
370	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
371	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
372	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
373	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
374	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
375	\end{lemma}
376	
377	\begin{proof}
378	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
379	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
380	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
381	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
382	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
383	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
384	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
385	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
386	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
387	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
388	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
389	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
390	\end{proof}
391	
392	\begin{remark}
393	[The full monoidal category on all of $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$ is off-path; the group law consumes only propositions]
394	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
395	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
396	        lem:whisker_of_W}
397	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
398	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
399	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
400	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
401	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
402	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. This is explicitly \emph{off-path}
403	  and \emph{not pursued}. One could in principle obtain it as the output of
404	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
405	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
406	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), supplying the instance
407	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}); but the group law never
408	  consumes a coherent monoidal category, so this construction is superseded and not
409	  carried out. The substrate provides only the \emph{existence} of the three
410	  coherence isomorphisms that the group law actually consumes.
411	
412	  Were that superseded route pursued, the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\)
413	  would need \emph{no} closed structure: its
414	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
415	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
416	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} (a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
417	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
418	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\)), the same technique Mathlib uses for
419	  fixed-base presheaves in
420	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\). It would use neither
421	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
422	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
423	  false over a non-affine open and is likewise off the critical path). The \emph{full}
424	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance and the \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal
425	  category are not on the critical path: the group law is realized without them. The
426	  single left-whisker existence lemma and its underlying d.1/d.2 stalk apparatus, by
427	  contrast, \emph{are} on the critical path, since the associator's sheafification
428	  transport consumes exactly that one whisker obligation
429	  (\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk}, closed via d.2).
430	
431	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
432	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
433	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
434	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
435	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
436	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
437	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
438	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
439	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
440	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, supplied directly: the associator by gluing canonical
441	  local isomorphisms through the restriction-compatibility isomorphism
442	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), and the
443	  unitors and braiding by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern
444	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). The carrier --- iso-classes
445	  of the substrate tensor \(\otimes_X\) --- is the scheme-theoretic analogue of
446	  Mathlib's Picard group \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(
447	  \mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}), but it
448	  is built \emph{differently}: Mathlib reads its \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) off the
449	  \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent monoidal category

3301	  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}
3302	  The map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) is built (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}) by
3303	  transporting the abstract oplax comparison \(\delta\) of the presheaf pullback
3304	  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}) through the sheafification
3305	  reconciliations. Each ingredient is natural: the abstract oplax \(\delta\) is natural
3306	  in both arguments --- this is Mathlib's
3307	  \(\mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.}\delta\mathtt{\_natural}\), valid for any oplax
3308	  monoidal functor --- and the sheafification, the \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)
3309	  comparison, and the unit/tensor reconciliation \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) are
3310	  natural transformations. The asserted square is the pasting of these naturality squares,
3311	  so it commutes; naturality in the two arguments is checked separately and then combined,
3312	  the tensor of the two component morphisms factoring as \(f^*a \otimes f^*b\) by
3313	  bifunctoriality of \(\otimes_Y\).
3314	\end{proof}

3319	[The comparison on the unit pair $(\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})$ is an isomorphism (D2$'$)]
3320	  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit}
3321	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso}
3322	  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:isiso_pullbacktensormap_of_sheafifydelta}
3323	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes. On the monoidal unit pair
3324	  \((\mathcal{O}_X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) the sheaf-level comparison
3325	  \[
3326	    \delta_{(\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})} :
3327	      f^*(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X)
3328	      \;\longrightarrow\;
3329	      f^*\mathcal{O}_X \otimes_Y f^*\mathcal{O}_X
3330	  \]
3331	  of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map} is an isomorphism.
3332	\end{lemma}

3334	\begin{proof}
3335	  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:isiso_pullbacktensormap_of_sheafifydelta, lem:isiso_sheafifyeta_of_unitsquare, lem:eta_bridge_unit_square}
3336	  By \cref{lem:isiso_pullbacktensormap_of_sheafifydelta} applied on the unit pair,
3337	  it suffices to verify that the sheafified presheaf comparison
3338	  \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}\,(\delta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\,\mathbf{1}\,\mathbf{1})\)
3339	  is an isomorphism; the underlying presheaf of the structure-sheaf unit coincides with
3340	  the presheaf monoidal unit \(\mathbf{1}\).
3341	
3342	  On the unit pair the presheaf-level oplax comparison \(\delta\) is governed by Mathlib's
3343	  oplax-monoidal \emph{unit coherence}: \(\mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.left\_unitality\_hom}\)
3344	  expresses \(\delta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\,\mathbf{1}\,\mathbf{1}\) as a composite of
3345	  the functorial unitor \(F.\mathrm{map}\,(\lambda_{\mathbf{1}}).\mathrm{hom}\), the target
3346	  unitor \(\lambda_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf{1}}\), and the whiskered presheaf unit comparison
3347	  \(\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi') \rhd F.\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf{1}\). The unitors are

3351	  \]

3353	  \emph{unit square}

3355	    (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv} \mathbin{;}
3356	      a_Y.\mathrm{map}\,(\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')) \mathbin{;}
3357	      \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom}

3359	  \]

3361	  \cref{lem:isiso_sheafifyeta_of_unitsquare} yields
3362	  \(\mathtt{IsIso}\bigl(a_Y.\mathrm{map}\,(\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'))\bigr)\) --- the
3363	  right-hand side \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,\varphi = \mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f :
3364	  f^*\mathcal{O}_X \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_Y\) being an isomorphism for \emph{every}

3369	\subsection{The unit square (D2$'$): a mate-calculus telescope}
3370	
3371	The proof of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit} bottoms out in the single morphism equation
3372	labelled the \emph{unit square} below. Its proof is a telescope across three nested adjunction
3373	layers (sheafification, presheaf pullback--pushforward, sheaf pullback--pushforward). This
3374	subsection isolates the load-bearing intermediate identities as named lemmas so each can be
3375	discharged independently, then assembles them.
3376	
3377	We fix throughout a morphism of schemes \(f : Y \to X\) and write
3378	\(\varphi = f.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\), \(\varphi' = \varphi.\mathrm{hom}\). Let \(a_X, a_Y\)
3379	denote sheafification of presheaves of modules on \(X\), \(Y\); \(\mathbf{1}^{\mathrm{p}}\) the
3380	presheaf monoidal unit; \(F = \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'\) the presheaf pullback;
3381	\(\eta\,F : F.\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf{1}^{\mathrm{p}} \to \mathbf{1}^{\mathrm{p}}\) its oplax unit
3382	comparison; and \(\varepsilon(G)\) the lax unit comparison of a lax monoidal functor \(G\). The
3383	isomorphisms \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,\mathcal{O}_X\) and \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\) are
3384	the file's comparison morphisms relating the sheaf-level pullback value to the sheafification of
3385	the presheaf-level pullback. Write
3386	\(\mathtt{sheafCounit}_\bullet = (\mathtt{sheafificationAdjunction}\,(\mathbf{1}_{\bullet.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}.\mathrm{val}})).\mathrm{counit}\).
3387
